description: did:key resolution MUST reject a key whose multicodec prefix is neither 0xed01 (Ed25519) nor 0x1200 (P-256) with key_resolution_failed (RFC-ACDP-0001 §5.11.1 step 3). The DID below decodes successfully from base58-btc but carries the secp256k1 multicodec prefix 0xe701 over the sig-003 test key bytes — a well-formed did:key for a curve ACDP does not support.

input.scenario: Publish (or consumer-side verification) where agent_id / signature.key_id carry a did:key with multicodec prefix 0xe701 (secp256k1)

expected.behavior: Step 3 of the pure resolution algorithm: after multibase decoding, the prefix MUST be 0xed01 or 0x1200; 0xe701 MUST be rejected with key_resolution_failed (permanent — the producer must re-key, not retry). The registry MUST NOT fall back to treating the remaining bytes as a raw key, and MUST NOT report unsupported_algorithm (signature.algorithm may be perfectly supported; the identity encoding is what is invalid).
